detail:
The code is exactly as cited and the defect is real. RuntimeDailyNoteAppendView.post (apps/integrations/runtime_views.py:1287-1289) and RuntimeDocumentAppendView.post (2322-2323) both do an unguarded read-modify-write: get_or_create loads the full doc.markdown, the new entry is concatenated in Python, and doc.save() rewrites the whole field — with no transaction.atomic, no select_for_update, and no DB-side append. A grep confirms zero locking in either the integrations runtime path or the parallel apps/journal/document_views.py:418-419 DocumentAppendView, and Document.markdown (apps/journal/models.py:184) is a plain TextField with no version/optimistic-lock column. The model docstring even states "Both human and agent append to it," so concurrent writers is an expected pattern — the lost-update window is real: if two appends to the same daily note overlap, the second save() clobbers the first's appended entry, silently, with no error surfaced to the user.

The reviewer's concurrency premise largely holds. The per-tenant serialization that does exist (apps/router/pending_queue.py) is keyed by (tenant, channel, channel_user_id) and covers only INBOUND webhook messages — it does not serialize across channels (e.g. iOS app channel vs Telegram) and does not cover cron-triggered turns, which fire inside the container on OpenClaw's own scheduler (crons are registered via cron.add in apps/cron/gateway_client.py, not dispatched as Django turns). So two independent execution contexts (a cron-fired section write + a live chat quick-log append, or two distinct-channel turns) genuinely can arrive as overlapping HTTP appends to the same Document. The billing counters' F() usage (apps/billing/services.py) is a valid in-repo contrast showing the team already knows the safe pattern.

What holds severity to LOW rather than MEDIUM: the RMW window is one short DB round-trip (read row, write row in the same request), and it only loses data when two distinct writers hit the SAME daily-note row inside that narrow window. With the fleet at ~1 active tenant and appends being infrequent, the overlap is rare in practice — but it is a latent correctness bug that grows with scale and channel count, and silent data loss is the worst failure mode, so it is worth fixing.</final_detail>
<suggested_fix>Wrap each append in a transaction and re-read the row under a row lock so the read-modify-write is serialized at the DB. Minimal version: in both RuntimeDailyNoteAppendView.post and RuntimeDocumentAppendView.post, after get_or_create, do `with transaction.atomic(): doc = Document.objects.select_for_update().get(pk=doc.pk); <re-read doc.markdown>; doc.markdown = ...; doc.save(update_fields=["markdown", "updated_at"])`. Apply the same to apps/journal/document_views.py:418-419 DocumentAppendView for parity. (A pure F()-concat won't work cleanly here because the entry depends on rstrip() of the current value, so select_for_update is the right tool, mirroring the pending_queue locking pattern already used elsewhere in the repo.)</suggested_fix>
<evidence">apps/integrations/runtime_views.py:1287 doc.markdown = (doc.markdown or '').rstrip() + ... ; :1289 doc.save() — no atomic/lock</evidence>
</invoke>
evidence: no transaction.atomic / select_for_update anywhere in runtime_views.py (grep returned no matches)
apps/integrations/urls.py:157 daily-note/append/ and :224 document/append/ are separately-reachable runtime endpoints
